title: Participle Clauses
description: Reducing relative and adverbial clauses using present (-ing) or past (-ed) participles.
summary_pt_br: Particípio: reduz orações relativas. 'The man reading the paper' = 'The man WHO IS reading'.

scenes.subtitle_en: Reducing relative and adverbial clauses using present (-ing) or past (-ed) participles.
scenes.subtitle_pt: Particípio: reduz orações relativas. 'The man reading the paper' = 'The man WHO IS reading'.

scenes.text_en: Reducing relative and adverbial clauses using present (-ing) or past (-ed) participles. Today's focus: participle clauses. The student reading in the corner is my nephew.
scenes.text_pt: Particípio: reduz orações relativas. 'The man reading the paper' = 'The man WHO IS reading'. Foco de hoje: participle clauses. O aluno lendo no canto é meu sobrinho.

scenes.title: Participle Clauses
scenes.body_en: Active: 'The man WHO was reading the paper' → 'The man READING the paper'. Passive: 'The books WHICH WERE ordered' → 'The books ORDERED'. Use to keep writing concise.
scenes.body_pt: Particípio: reduz orações relativas. 'The man reading the paper' = 'The man WHO IS reading'.

examples.en: The student reading in the corner is my nephew.
examples.pt: O aluno lendo no canto é meu sobrinho.

examples.en: The books ordered last week arrived today.
examples.pt: Os livros pedidos semana passada chegaram.

examples.en: Having finished the report, she went home.
examples.pt: Tendo terminado o relatório, ela foi pra casa.

exercise.question: Complete: "The ___ reading in the corner is my nephew."
exercise.explanation: Full sentence: "The student reading in the corner is my nephew."
exercise.hint_pt_br: O aluno lendo no canto é meu sobrinho.

exercise.question: Which sentence is correct?
options: I haven't heard anything about that.
The books ordered last week arrived today.
exercise.explanation: The correct sentence is: "The books ordered last week arrived today."
exercise.hint_pt_br: Os livros pedidos semana passada chegaram.

exercise.explanation: Five key terms from the lesson. Match each English word to its Portuguese meaning.

scenes.title: Participle Clauses — dialog
scenes.pt_summary: Diálogo sobre participle clauses. Pratique as frases marcadas.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Let's reduce this sentence. 'The woman who is singing is my mother.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The woman singing is my mother.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now reduce: 'The letters that were sent yesterday arrived today.'

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: The letters sent yesterday arrived today.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Try a perfect participle. 'Because she had finished, she left.'

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now combine two short sentences using a participle.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Smiling kindly, he greeted every student.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Good. Formal register is cleaner with participles.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: My writing sounds more academic now.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Now you have the tool. Use it sparingly.

scenes.title: Editing a student essay
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que entendeu — resumo, detalhes e sua opinião. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Describe how Kumar improved the essay. Use at least two participle reductions.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que ouviu. Resumo curto com quem falou, o que disseram e sua opinião.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Kumar sat with a student whose essay was clear but far too long for the word limit.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: The phrase 'students who are learning English' can simply become 'students learning English'.

paragraphs.speaker: Student
paragraphs.en: Does reducing clauses change the meaning or just the length of the sentence?

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Participles used correctly keep every meaning while cutting the noise around it.

paragraphs.speaker: Kumar
paragraphs.en: Having read your essay twice, I can already see three places to tighten your argument.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: Good writing, Kumar repeated, is not what you add — it's what you have the courage to remove.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Participle Clauses". Reducing relative and adverbial clauses using present (-ing) or past (-ed) participles. The student reading in the corner is my nephew. The books ordered last week arrived today. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: Prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Participle Clauses". I liked it because it was clear and useful. See you next time!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule from last year

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para continuar praticando. Salve os links e volte sempre.

sources.label: BBC News · Learning English
sources.pt_hint: Análise de notícias reais da BBC.

sources.label: TED Talks with subtitles
sources.pt_hint: Palestras reais com legenda — ouça, leia, repita.

sources.label: Cambridge Dictionary · thesaurus
sources.pt_hint: Sinônimos com exemplos — essencial em B2+.
